---
title: "Computer Programming I (Python)"
subtitle: "An Introductory Python Course for Math Students"
---

## Welcome! 👋

Welcome to **Computer Programming I (Python)**, an introductory Python course designed specifically for Math students. This course will teach you the fundamentals of programming using Python, one of the most popular and versatile programming languages today.

## Course Overview

This course is structured into four comprehensive modules that will guide you from the basics of Python to more advanced programming concepts:

### Module 1: Python fundamentals
Learn the fundamentals of Python programming, including syntax, control flow, and functions.

### Module 2: Structures
Explore lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets and common sequence operations.

### Module 3: NumPy
Work with arrays, vectorized operations, and basic numerical workflows useful for math students.

### Module 4: IDEs & Tools
Learn to use Jupyter, VS Code, and debugging tools; manage environments and packages.

## Getting Started

::: {.callout-tip}
## Prerequisites

- No prior programming experience required!
- A computer with internet access
- Enthusiasm to learn
:::

To get started with the course:

1. Review the [Syllabus](syllabus.qmd) for course details and schedule
2. Begin with [Module 1: Python fundamentals](modules/python-fundamentals/_index.qmd)
3. Work through each module sequentially
4. Complete the exercises and check your solutions

## Learning Approach

Each lesson includes:

- **Theory**: Clear explanations of concepts
- **Examples**: Practical code examples you can run
- **Exercises**: Practice problems to test your understanding
- **Solutions**: Detailed solutions with explanations

::: {.callout-note}
## Interactive Learning

All code examples in this course can be copied and run in your own Python environment. We encourage you to experiment with the code and make it your own!
:::

## Need Help?

If you encounter any issues or have questions:

- Review the lesson materials carefully
- Try the exercises before looking at solutions
- Check the course repository for updates
- Reach out to the instructor or teaching assistants

Happy coding! 🐍
